title: View not created in default schema cannot be queried from other schema
description:
### What happens?

If a view is not created in default schema and the relation name in view's definition is not qualified with its schema, the view cannot be queried in any other schema.

### To Reproduce

```sql
D create schema new;
D use new;
D CREATE TABLE t1 AS SELECT 42 AS i, 84 AS j;
D create view v as (select * from t1);
D select * from v;  -- ok
┌───────┬───────┐
│   i   │   j   │
│ int32 │ int32 │
├───────┼───────┤
│    42 │    84 │
└───────┴───────┘
D use main;
D select * from new.v;  -- error
Catalog Error: Table with name t1 does not exist!
Did you mean "new.t1"?
```

### OS:

x86_64

### DuckDB Version:

1.1.13

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Wei

### Affiliation:

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_schema.t1 (id INTEGER, name TEXT, value REAL, created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_schema.t1 VALUES (1, 'test', 42.0, NULL), (2, 'example', NULL, '2023-10-01 12:00:00');
CREATE VIEW test_schema.v AS SELECT id, name, COALESCE(value, 0) AS value FROM test_schema.t1;
key_bug_features: View in non-default schema cannot be queried
